walkthrough.title: Open the Companion App
walkthrough.text: Let's get Forgotten Waters set up. The very first thing you need is the companion app. Open it on a tablet, laptop, or phone. The app is web-based, so no download is required. It will narrate the story, play music, and track your adventure.

walkthrough.title: Select a Scenario
walkthrough.text: In the app, select the scenario you want to play. If this is your first game, choose the introductory scenario. The app will guide you through scenario-specific setup steps, so keep it handy throughout.

walkthrough.title: Place the Ship Board
walkthrough.text: Now let's set up the physical table. Place the ship board in the center where everyone can reach it. This board tracks your ship's five vital stats: hull, supplies, crew, guns, and sails. Set each stat to the starting value the app tells you.

walkthrough.title: Place the Map Board
walkthrough.text: Set the map board next to the ship board. This represents the ocean your crew will explore. The app will tell you where to place your ship standee and any starting location tokens on the map.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Shared Tokens
walkthrough.text: Place the supply tokens, treasure tokens, skill tokens, and dice in the center of the table where everyone can reach them. These are shared resources you will use throughout the adventure.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Pirate
walkthrough.text: Each player selects a pirate character and takes the matching character sheet and story book. Give your pirate a name and mark your starting stats as directed by the app. Your story book contains numbered entries that will unfold based on your choices during the game.

walkthrough.title: Take Your Action Tokens
walkthrough.text: Each player takes a set of action tokens in their chosen color. You will place these tokens on action spaces each round to show what your pirate is doing. Also grab a pirate meeple in your color.

walkthrough.title: Assign the First Mate
walkthrough.text: The app will assign one player the role of first mate. This player manages turn flow and reads certain app prompts aloud. The first mate role may rotate during the game.

walkthrough.title: Read the Opening Narration
walkthrough.text: Let the app play the opening narration. This sets the stage for your pirate adventure. Listen carefully because it often contains hints about what your crew will face. Everyone settled in? Let's learn how to play.

walkthrough.title: Scenario-Specific Steps
walkthrough.text: Finally, follow any additional setup instructions the app provides. These vary by scenario and might include placing special tokens, setting story flags, or adjusting ship stats. The app walks you through each step one at a time.

summary.title: Open the Companion App
bullets: Open the web-based companion app on a tablet or laptop
App provides narration, music, and story tracking

summary.title: Select a Scenario
bullets: Choose a scenario in the app (intro scenario for first game)
App will guide scenario-specific setup

summary.title: Place the Ship Board
bullets: Ship board tracks hull, supplies, crew, guns, sails
Set starting values as directed by the app

summary.title: Place the Map Board
bullets: Map board represents the ocean
Place ship standee and starting location tokens per app

summary.title: Prepare Shared Tokens
bullets: Supply tokens, treasure tokens, skill tokens, dice in center
Shared resources accessible to all players

summary.title: Choose Your Pirate
bullets: Each player picks a pirate character + story book
Name your pirate, mark starting stats per app

summary.title: Take Your Action Tokens
bullets: Each player takes action tokens in their color
Also take a pirate meeple in matching color

bullets: App assigns one player as first mate

summary.title: Read the Opening Narration

summary.title: Scenario-Specific Steps
bullets: Follow any additional app-driven setup instructions
May include special tokens, story flags, or stat adjustments

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Forgotten Waters is a cooperative narrative adventure for three to seven players. You are all pirates on a shared ship, living out a branching story driven by the companion app. Your goal is to keep the ship afloat while advancing your personal pirate stories.

walkthrough.title: Round Structure
walkthrough.text: Each round of play has five phases: Narrative, Planning, Action, Ship, and Navigation. The app guides you through each phase in order. A round represents a chunk of time in your adventure, and the number of rounds varies by scenario.

walkthrough.title: The Narrative Phase
walkthrough.text: The app presents a story scene with voice-acted narration. Listen carefully and discuss the situation with your crew. The app may present choices that the group votes on together.

walkthrough.title: The Planning Phase
walkthrough.text: The app shows a location page with numbered action spaces, each with limited slots. Players discuss where to place their action tokens, then everyone places simultaneously. Available actions include things like explore, forage, repair, train, and advance your personal story.

walkthrough.title: The Action Phase
walkthrough.text: Now we resolve actions in numbered order. The app guides each action space one at a time. Some actions require skill checks, where you roll dice and add your skill bonus to meet a target number. Some actions trigger story entries in your personal story book.

walkthrough.title: Skill Checks
walkthrough.text: When one is required, roll the specified number of dice and add your relevant skill bonus from your character sheet. If you meet or exceed the target number, you succeed. The app or card tells you what happens on success or failure.

walkthrough.title: Ship Stats Explained
walkthrough.text: Your ship has five vital stats that the whole crew manages together. Hull is your ship's health. If it hits zero, the ship sinks and everyone loses. Supplies are food and resources. Crew represents your pirate NPCs. Guns determine combat strength. Sails affect your speed and navigation options.

walkthrough.title: The Ship Phase
walkthrough.text: After all actions resolve, the Ship Phase begins. Your crew consumes supplies, so the supply track goes down. The app may trigger events like storms, encounters, or story beats. If any ship stat drops to zero, bad things happen immediately.

walkthrough.title: The Navigation Phase
walkthrough.text: If the scenario requires it, your crew decides where to sail next on the map. Move the ship standee to the chosen location and continue the adventure into the next round.

walkthrough.title: Personal Stories
walkthrough.text: Each pirate has a story book with numbered entries. By choosing story actions and making decisions, you unlock entries that advance your personal narrative. You need to complete enough of your story to achieve your pirate's happy ending by the game's conclusion.

walkthrough.title: The Constellation Track
walkthrough.text: Each player has a constellation track that fills in over the course of the game. Reaching certain thresholds on this track unlocks abilities and advances your story progress. Think of it as your personal progress meter.

walkthrough.title: Timed Decisions
walkthrough.text: Some decisions presented by the app have timers. When the timer appears, your crew must decide quickly or the app will choose a default option for you. The default is usually bad, so communicate fast and make a call.

walkthrough.title: Cooperative but Personal
walkthrough.text: Here is an important nuance. The game is cooperative in that if the ship sinks or the scenario objective fails, everyone loses. But each player also has a personal win condition based on their story. It is possible for the group to survive but individual pirates to fail their personal stories.

walkthrough.title: How the Game Ends
walkthrough.text: The scenario ends when the story reaches its conclusion as determined by the app. If the ship survived and the main objective was completed, the group succeeds. Then each player checks their constellation track and story progress to see if they achieved their personal happy ending. There is no point scoring. This is a narrative experience with pass or fail outcomes.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Cooperative narrative adventure for 3-7 players
App-driven branching pirate story
Keep the ship afloat + advance personal pirate stories

walkthrough.title: Welcome Aboard
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through the first round of a scenario with a crew of four example pirates. We will cover each phase so you see how the game flows. Even if your crew size is different, follow along to learn the rhythm.

walkthrough.title: The App Starts Your Story
walkthrough.text: The app plays an opening narration. Our pirates are on a rickety ship fleeing a naval patrol. Listen to the narration together. The app sets the mood with music and voice acting. Lean into it and enjoy the theatrics.

walkthrough.title: Narrative Phase: A Choice Appears
walkthrough.text: The app presents your first group decision. Do you sail toward the mysterious island or try to outrun the patrol? The crew discusses. There is no mathematically correct answer. This is a story game, so pick what sounds interesting. Let's say the crew votes to head for the island.

walkthrough.title: Planning Phase: Reading the Location
walkthrough.text: The app directs you to a location page with action spaces. You see six numbered spaces: Explore the Shore, Forage for Supplies, Repair the Hull, Stand Watch, Train Your Skills, and Advance Your Story. Each space has a limited number of slots, usually one or two.

walkthrough.title: Planning Phase: Placing Tokens
walkthrough.text: Now the crew discusses who goes where. Pirate 1 wants to explore. Pirate 2 says the hull is dangerously low, so she will repair. Pirate 3 wants to advance his story. Pirate 4 will forage for supplies. Everyone places their action tokens simultaneously on their chosen spaces.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Resolving Space One
walkthrough.text: Let's resolve the actions in order. Space one is Explore the Shore. Pirate 1 needs to pass a skill check. She rolls two dice and adds her navigation skill bonus. The target is eight. She rolls a five and a three, plus her bonus of two, for a total of ten. Success! The app narrates a discovery.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Repair and Forage
walkthrough.text: Space two is Repair the Hull. Pirate 2 passes her skill check and the hull goes up by two. Space three is Forage. Pirate 4 rolls and succeeds, gaining three supplies for the ship. These keep the crew fed and the ship running.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Personal Story
walkthrough.text: Space four is Advance Your Story. Pirate 3 reads the entry number the app tells him in his personal story book. A thrilling scene unfolds about his pirate's past. His constellation track advances one notch. This is how you progress toward your personal happy ending.

walkthrough.title: Ship Phase: Consuming Supplies
walkthrough.text: Now the Ship Phase begins. The crew eats, so the supply track drops by the number the app specifies. Then the app checks for events. A storm is brewing. The app tells you the sails stat is tested, and the crew just barely passes. Close call.

walkthrough.title: Navigation Phase: Where To Next
walkthrough.text: The Navigation Phase arrives. The map shows two possible destinations. The crew debates and decides to sail toward a harbor town. Move the ship standee to the new location on the map. Round one is complete.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Ignoring the Ship
walkthrough.text: In our next round, all four pirates choose personal story actions because the narrative is exciting. Nobody repairs or forages. The hull drops from events and supplies run out. The crew is now in crisis mode. If the hull hits zero, the ship sinks and everyone loses.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Balance Ship and Story
walkthrough.text: The takeaway is that you must balance ship maintenance with personal story advancement. At least one or two pirates should handle ship duties each round. Rotate who does the grunt work so nobody falls too far behind on their personal narrative.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Saving Your Story for Later
walkthrough.text: Pirate 3 spent every round on ship duties and never touched his story book. The scenario ends abruptly and he has not completed enough entries for his happy ending.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Advance Your Story Steadily
walkthrough.text: Do not procrastinate on your personal story. The scenario can end sooner than you expect, and you cannot rush through story entries at the last minute. Aim to advance your story at least a little each round or every other round.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Not Communicating
walkthrough.text: In one round, Pirate 1 and Pirate 4 both want the single repair slot. They place tokens simultaneously without discussing it, and one pirate is blocked. That pirate wasted their turn. Communication during the planning phase is essential.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Talk Before You Place
walkthrough.text: Always discuss your plans before placing action tokens. The limited slots mean coordination matters. Call out which spaces you need and negotiate who goes where. A few seconds of planning prevents wasted turns.

walkthrough.title: Round Two and Beyond
walkthrough.text: From here, each round follows the same five-phase pattern. The app narrates, you plan, you act, the ship consumes resources, and you navigate. The story branches based on your collective choices. Every playthrough creates a unique adventure.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen all five phases, watched the crew handle skill checks, and learned three common mistakes to avoid. Remember to balance ship duties with story progress, advance your story steadily, and communicate with your crew.

walkthrough.title: Before You Set Sail
walkthrough.text:
Before you embark on a full scenario, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics on keeping your ship afloat.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to ship stats and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest traps new crews fall into. First, neglecting ship maintenance because the story is too exciting. Second, leaving personal story progress to the very end. Third, not communicating during the planning phase, which leads to wasted turns. Fourth, panicking when the app throws a curveball instead of discussing it calmly. Keep these in mind and your crew will thrive.

walkthrough.title: Balance Ship and Story
walkthrough.text: The most important strategic principle is balance. Every round, your crew needs to split between ship maintenance actions and personal story actions. A good rule of thumb is that at least one third of the crew should handle ship duties each round. Rotate who takes on those duties so no single pirate sacrifices their entire game for the group.

walkthrough.title: Play to Your Strengths
walkthrough.text: Each pirate has different skill bonuses. When a skill check is required, the pirate with the highest relevant skill should ideally be the one attempting it. If Pirate 2 has a plus three in navigation and Pirate 4 has a plus one, send Pirate 2 to handle the navigation challenge. Matching skills to actions dramatically improves your success rate.

walkthrough.title: Keep a Supplies Buffer
walkthrough.text: Always try to keep your supplies at least two or three points above zero. Running out of supplies triggers cascading problems that can spiral out of control. It is much easier to forage proactively than to recover from a supply crisis. Think of supplies as your safety net.

walkthrough.title: Listen to the Narration
walkthrough.text: The companion app often hides hints and clues in the narration text. If the narrator mentions a rocky shoreline, that might hint at hull damage ahead. If a character mentions treasure, there might be a rewarding action space next round. Pay attention and discuss what you hear with your crew.

walkthrough.title: Advance Your Story Every Round
walkthrough.text: Make it a goal to advance your personal story at least every other round. The constellation track has thresholds that unlock abilities, and falling behind makes it very hard to catch up. Do not assume you will have time later. Scenarios can end more abruptly than you expect.

walkthrough.title: Embrace the Experience
walkthrough.text: Forgotten Waters is fundamentally a storytelling game. Making the optimal strategic choice every time is less important than making choices that create interesting stories. If a risky option sounds hilarious or dramatic, lean into it. The memories you create at the table are what make this game special.

walkthrough.title: Handle Timed Decisions Quickly
walkthrough.text: When the app presents a timed decision, do not freeze. The default option when the timer runs out is usually bad. Designate one crew member to make the final call if the group cannot agree in time. A quick imperfect decision beats a slow perfect one that never happens.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't neglect ship maintenance for story excitement
Don't leave personal story progress to the end
Communicate during planning — wasted turns are costly
Stay calm when the app throws curveballs

walkthrough.title: Action Space Priority
walkthrough.text: Not all action spaces are created equal. Some spaces have immediate high-value outcomes, while others build toward future payoffs. In the early rounds, prioritize actions that improve your ship stats and character skills. In the mid to late game, shift toward story advancement and treasure hunting. Read each space description carefully before placing your token.

walkthrough.title: Skill Specialization
walkthrough.text: Each pirate should invest in becoming excellent at one or two skills rather than being mediocre at everything. When you train, focus on skills that complement your role. If you are the designated navigator, push that skill as high as possible. Specialist crews pass skill checks far more reliably than generalist ones.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Scenario Rhythm
walkthrough.text: Every scenario has a natural rhythm. Early rounds tend to focus on exploration and resource gathering. Mid-game introduces complications and branching choices. The final rounds ramp up with climactic story beats. Once you recognize this rhythm, you can anticipate when to shift from maintenance to story focus.

walkthrough.title: Constellation Track Optimization
walkthrough.text: Your constellation track unlocks abilities at specific thresholds. Study your track early and plan which thresholds to hit and when. Some thresholds provide immediate combat bonuses, while others unlock story entries. Prioritize thresholds that synergize with the current scenario's demands.

walkthrough.title: Ship Stat Management
walkthrough.text: Advanced crews manage ship stats proactively rather than reactively. Keep hull above half whenever possible. Monitor supplies two rounds ahead. Guns and sails matter more in some scenarios than others, so adjust your priorities based on what the story seems to be building toward. A well-maintained ship gives you the freedom to focus on story.

walkthrough.title: Group Decision Framework
walkthrough.text: When the app presents a group choice, use a quick framework. Ask three questions: What are the likely consequences? Which option aligns with our current needs? And which option makes for the best story? If the crew is healthy and the ship is strong, take the risky option for a better narrative. If you are struggling, play it safe.

walkthrough.title: Replayability and Branching
walkthrough.text: Forgotten Waters scenarios have extensive branching paths. On a second playthrough, deliberately make different choices to explore alternate story branches. The game is designed to reward curiosity. You will discover entirely new story beats, locations, and character endings by choosing the paths you skipped the first time.

walkthrough.title: Maximizing Personal Endings
walkthrough.text: To achieve your personal happy ending, you need to complete enough constellation track milestones and story book entries. The fastest path is to take story actions whenever the ship is stable and to train your skills so that story-related skill checks succeed consistently. Coordinate with your crew so that everyone gets enough story turns without the ship falling apart.

Read space descriptions before placing tokens

Specialist crews pass checks more reliably

entries.definition: The ship's structural health tracked on the ship board. If hull reaches zero, the ship sinks and all players lose the game immediately.
Food and resources consumed by the crew each round. If supplies reach zero, crew morale drops and cascading negative events trigger.
The number of NPC pirates aboard the ship. Losing crew reduces the ship's overall effectiveness and may limit available actions.
The ship's combat strength, used during naval encounters and combat events. Higher guns improve outcomes in battle scenarios.
The ship's speed and maneuverability. Higher sails affect navigation options and may help avoid negative events.
The web-based application required to play Forgotten Waters. It narrates the story, plays music, presents choices, tracks story progression, and guides each phase of every round.
The central board tracking the five ship stats: hull, supplies, crew, guns, and sails. All players share responsibility for maintaining these stats.
The ocean exploration board where the ship standee moves between locations. The app directs which locations are available for navigation.
Each player's personal pirate record showing their name, skill bonuses, constellation track, and story progress.
A personal booklet for each pirate character containing numbered story entries. Entries are read when directed by the app during story actions.
Colored discs each player places on action spaces during the Planning Phase to indicate their chosen action for the round.
The first phase of each round. The app presents a story scene with voice-acted narration and may offer group decisions.
The second phase where players simultaneously place action tokens on available action spaces at the current location.
The third phase where action spaces are resolved in numbered order. May involve skill checks, story entries, or ship stat changes.
The fourth phase where the crew consumes supplies and the app may trigger events like storms or encounters.
The fifth and final phase where the crew decides where to sail next on the map board.
A dice roll plus a relevant skill bonus compared to a target number. Meeting or exceeding the target means success. The app or card resolves the outcome.
A personal progress meter on each character sheet that fills in during the game. Reaching thresholds unlocks abilities and advances story progress.
Each pirate's unique narrative arc tracked through their story book. Completing enough story entries and constellation milestones earns a personal happy ending.
A pirate's personal win condition achieved by completing sufficient constellation track progress and story book entries by game end.
An app-driven choice with a countdown timer. If the crew does not decide before time runs out, the app selects a default option, which is usually unfavorable.
A rotating player role assigned by the app. The first mate manages turn flow and reads certain prompts aloud to the group.
A self-contained adventure lasting two to four hours with its own story, objectives, and setup instructions. Each scenario offers a unique narrative experience.
Story decisions that lead to different narrative outcomes. Replaying the same scenario with different choices produces very different stories.
The shared win condition: the ship survives and the main scenario objective is completed. If the ship sinks or the objective fails, everyone loses.
Each player's personal win condition based on constellation track and story progress. A player can achieve group success but fail their personal story, or vice versa.
An action type that discovers new locations, items, or story elements on the map.
An action type that gains supplies for the ship, keeping the crew fed and the supply track healthy.
An action type that restores hull points to the ship, preventing the ship from sinking.
An action type that improves your pirate's skill bonuses, making future skill checks easier.
